title: E5 architecture misses — S0 governance gate + S1/S2/S3 additions

issues.title: Draft Confidence Model & Governance Gate — implement first, dogfood during E5
issues.scope: internal/ops/types.go, internal/materialize/materialize.go, internal/ready/compute.go, internal/decompose/apply.go, internal/tui/dagsummary/model.go, cmd/trellis/dag_summary.go, cmd/trellis/main_test.go
issues.dod: decompose-apply creates draft nodes; draft nodes excluded from ready queue; dag-summary interactive sign-off promotes draft to verified; make check green
notes: Architecture section 9: draft confidence model is the governance gate for all decomposition work
Dogfood by running E5's own dag-summary sign-off after this story lands
All other E5 stories are blocked on this in spirit — work on them only after S0 is done and signed off

issues.title: Confidence field in materialized state and draft gate in ready queue
issues.dod: draft nodes excluded from arm ready; verified nodes appear normally; existing nodes without confidence field default to verified; tests pass; make check green
notes: Add ConfidenceDraft, ConfidenceVerified, ConfidenceInferred constants to ops/types.go
Default empty confidence to verified for backward compat with pre-E5 issues
Commit: feat(governance): add draft confidence field and ready-gate for unverified nodes

issues.title: `dag-transition` op — materialization and thin CLI command
issues.dod: dag-transition op walks subtree and promotes all draft nodes to verified; arm dag-transition CLI command emits the op; tests pass; make check green
notes: Op format: [dag-transition, root_id, ts, worker_id, {to: approved, uncovered_acknowledged: [...]}]
Walk subtree rooted at target_id; set confidence=verified on all draft descendants
Commit: feat(governance): add dag-transition op promoting draft subtree to verified

issues.title: `dag-summary` interactive sign-off TUI — per-item acknowledgment, emits dag-transition
issues.dod: dag-summary launches BubbleTea checklist; all items must be actioned (y/n/s) before sign-off unlocks; uncited nodes require explicit ID acknowledgment; sign-off emits dag-transition op; q without full sign-off emits no ops; make check green
notes: Architecture section 9: mechanically impossible to bulk-approve
Per-item: y=approve, n=flag for re-decomposition, s=skip/defer
Coverage summary at bottom: cited N/M nodes (X%)
Commit: feat(governance): wire dag-summary interactive sign-off TUI (architecture section 9)

issues.title: `decompose-apply` emits `confidence: draft` on all created nodes
issues.dod: All nodes created by decompose-apply have confidence=draft; they do not appear in arm ready until dag-summary sign-off; dogfood test passes; make check green
notes: Include confidence:draft in every create op payload in ApplyPlan
Dogfood: run decompose-apply on a test plan, run dag-summary, confirm ready queue populates
Commit: feat(governance): decompose-apply emits draft confidence; requires dag-summary sign-off to claim

issues.title: `decompose-apply` missing architecture flags — `--root`, `--generate-ids`, `--strict`
issues.dod: --strict treats advisory warnings as errors; --generate-ids replaces plan IDs with UUIDs; --root overrides inferred root; tests pass; make check green
notes: Architecture section 7 specifies these flags — they were always in scope, just missed
Commit: feat(ux): add decompose-apply --root, --generate-ids, --strict (architecture section 7)

issues.title: `arm ready` interactive TUI in human mode — task selection and direct claim
issues.dod: arm ready on TTY launches BubbleTea selection list; enter claims selected task; --format=json bypasses TUI entirely; make check green
notes: Architecture section 15: ready should be interactive TUI for humans, JSON for agents
Distinct from arm tui kanban board — this is the task-selection + claim flow
Commit: feat(tui): add ready interactive TUI for human task selection and claim (architecture section 15)

issues.title: `arm materialize --exclude-worker <worker-id>` for selective forensic replay
issues.dod: materialize --exclude-worker <id> skips all ops from that worker's log; diagnostic only (no checkpoint update); tests pass; make check green
notes: Architecture section 19: selective replay for diagnosing whether a specific worker introduced corruption
Read-only diagnostic — does not modify ops branch or checkpoint
Commit: feat(forensics): add materialize --exclude-worker for selective replay (architecture section 19)
